steps: Cordon the node to prevent new scheduling.
Wait for checkpoint-safe jobs to reach a safe pause point when available.
Drain or reschedule the impacted jobs to alternate capacity.
Run DCGM diagnostics and capture support bundles.
Escalate to hardware operations if diagnostics fail or the alert recurs.
templates.estimated_resolution: 2-4 hours for diagnostics; 24-48 hours if replacement is required.
templates.rollback_plan: Uncordon the node and return workloads after diagnostics pass and post-check telemetry stays healthy for the observation window.
templates.approval_guidance: Requires human approval during peak hours or when the blast radius exceeds policy thresholds.

steps: Leave the node in service.
Capture telemetry and note recurrence counters.
Review workload logs for application-level explanations.
Escalate only if additional evidence raises severity.
templates.rollback_plan: No rollback needed because the node remains in service; revert only any temporary alert suppression or notification routing changes.
templates.approval_guidance: Can be auto-approved when confidence is low but the risk to workloads is minimal.

steps: Cordon the node if jobs are already failing; otherwise leave it schedulable until the approved maintenance window.
Notify affected users and the on-call operations channel.
Drain the node during the next maintenance window.
Run diagnostics and hardware inspection before returning the node to service.
templates.estimated_resolution: Next maintenance window plus 2-4 hours of diagnostics.
templates.rollback_plan: If diagnostics pass and urgency is downgraded, uncordon the node and resume normal scheduling under heightened monitoring.
templates.approval_guidance: Use when there is a hardware hypothesis but safety policy blocks immediate drain.

steps: Page the human operator or incident commander.
Preserve logs, telemetry snapshots, and job metadata.
Hold off on disruptive node actions until a human reviews the evidence.
templates.estimated_resolution: Depends on operator response and the scope of the incident.
templates.rollback_plan: No rollback is required because the action is investigative; document any temporary mitigations and revert them after review.
templates.approval_guidance: Mandatory when diagnosis confidence is below the policy threshold or when automated action would exceed a safety gate.

steps: Confirm the failed unit name from the alert metadata.
Restart the failed systemd unit on the affected node.
Verify the unit returns to an active state and exporter metrics resume.
Monitor for repeat failures before returning the incident to closed status.
templates.estimated_resolution: 10-20 minutes for service recovery and observation.
templates.rollback_plan: Stop the restarted unit and restore the previous service configuration or package version if the restart introduces instability.
templates.approval_guidance: Usually auto-approved because the action is localized and non-disruptive.

steps: Tag the node as unhealthy to block new workload placement.
Capture support diagnostics and host telemetry.
Terminate or replace the node according to infrastructure policy.
Open a follow-up ticket for hardware inspection and root-cause tracking.
templates.estimated_resolution: 30-90 minutes for node replacement plus follow-up diagnostics.
templates.rollback_plan: Remove the unhealthy tag and reprovision or return the node to service only if follow-up diagnostics clear the hardware suspicion.
templates.approval_guidance: Requires human approval during peak hours or when the blast radius exceeds policy thresholds.
